title: introduce cutover-marker and sequence IDs to stabilize SSE handoff and JSON completion
preview: Motivation: Intermittent production incidents showed that when a model switch or function/tool-call injection occurs mid-stream, downstream SSE consumers occasionally saw truncated JSON objects or duplicate sentinel events. Root causes included: (1) partial-object chunks being flushed out-of-order by proxies during ...
text:
Motivation: Intermittent production incidents showed that when a model switch or function/tool-call injection occurs mid-stream, downstream SSE consumers occasionally saw truncated JSON objects or duplicate sentinel events. Root causes included: (1) partial-object chunks being flushed out-of-order by proxies during cutover, (2) ambiguous empty-event delimiters emitted by the compatibility shim, and (3) lack of an idempotent reassembly key for retries and reconnects.

This change introduces two coordinated primitives to make handoff deterministic and safe: a cutover-marker event and a sequenced fragment ID (sequence_id + fragment_index) attached to every SSE data event emitted by the compat layer. The cutover-marker is a dedicated SSE event emitted immediately before a model/variant switch or when the runtime needs to inject a tool/function-call sentinel mid-stream. Consumers can use t...

title: Align error shapes with SDK taxonomy (request_id, code, retryable)
preview: Context: As part of the SDK parity initiative, we need consistent error shapes across all endpoint paths so Python/TS/Go can reliably classify retryability, surface request_id, and respect Retry-After. Today the OpenAI-compat layer returns a mix of OpenAI-style error bodies and legacy gateway bodies depending on rou...
text:
Context: As part of the SDK parity initiative, we need consistent error shapes across all endpoint paths so Python/TS/Go can reliably classify retryability, surface request_id, and respect Retry-After. Today the OpenAI-compat layer returns a mix of OpenAI-style error bodies and legacy gateway bodies depending on route (/v1/chat/completions vs /v1/embeddings vs /v1/responses compatibility shims), which causes SDK mismatches (e.g., TS retry middleware classifies based on error.code; Python expects request_id always present; Go relies on retryable flag + status). This PR aligns OpenAI-compat error formatting to the shared SDK error taxonomy while preserving OpenAI-compatible top-level structure for clients that parse `error.message`.

What this changes (high level): 1) Normalizes error response envelope for all OpenAI-compat routes to include `error` + Redwood fields: `request_id` and `e...

title: fix-sse-framing-boundary-loss-and-partial-json-emit
preview: Motivation: In production we observed a class of intermittent failures where streaming OpenAI-compatible responses (function calls / structured JSON) decoded incorrectly when the underlying SSE stream split in the middle of a JSON token or delimiter. Symptoms: user-facing truncated function_call arguments, invalid J...
text:
Motivation: In production we observed a class of intermittent failures where streaming OpenAI-compatible responses (function calls / structured JSON) decoded incorrectly when the underlying SSE stream split in the middle of a JSON token or delimiter. Symptoms: user-facing truncated function_call arguments, invalid JSON parse errors, and flakiness in downstream rerankers. Root cause: the SSE reassembly logic sometimes coalesced heartbeat/chunk delimiters and trimmed what looked like redundant newlines, which in edge cases removed the only event terminator for a partial 'data:' payload. That combined with naive prefix-buffering produced partial JSON emissions to the downstream decoder.

Summary of changes: 1) Introduce an explicit per-connection frame buffer state machine (frame_buffer_state) that guarantees we never drop the final event terminator; instead we canonicalize delimiters (C...

title: Align token counting + usage semantics with OpenAI-compatible endpoints
preview:
## Context
We’ve had recurring customer confusion + disputes where:
- Invoice/usage export values don’t match what they see in OpenAI-compatible responses under `usage`.
- Streaming requests (especially with tool/function calling) report inconsistent or inflated `completion_tokens`.
- Retries/fallbacks can cause the...
text:
## Context
We’ve had recurring customer confusion + disputes where:
- Invoice/usage export values don’t match what they see in OpenAI-compatible responses under `usage`.
- Streaming requests (especially with tool/function calling) report inconsistent or inflated `completion_tokens`.
- Retries/fallbacks can cause the compat layer to report usage for an attempt that wasn’t the billed attempt.

This PR aligns OpenAI-compatible `usage` reporting with Redwood’s internal token metering semantics for the Hosted API, using the same tokenization inputs we use for billing.

Relevant threads/tickets: SUP-28451 (invoice tokens do not match usage API), SUP-28463 (double-charged retries), ENG-4430 (double-counting on retry path), PM-1842 (meter sanity KPIs).

## What changes
### 1) Single source of truth for usage computation
- Introduces a dedicated `UsageCalculator` for OpenAI-compatible endpoint...

title: Optionally surface active optimization config version in response headers
preview: Context: As part of the Safe apply + rollback workstream, we need an easy way (especially for Support/SRE and advanced customers) to confirm which Optimization Config Version (batching/caching/quant profile) was active for a given request. Today, customers can infer this indirectly via dashboards or internal tooling...
text:
Context: As part of the Safe apply + rollback workstream, we need an easy way (especially for Support/SRE and advanced customers) to confirm which Optimization Config Version (batching/caching/quant profile) was active for a given request. Today, customers can infer this indirectly via dashboards or internal tooling, but it’s not visible at the request boundary.

What this PR does: Add an opt-in response header in the OpenAI-compatible proxy that surfaces the active optimization config version ID when available. This is intentionally disabled by default to avoid leaking internal identifiers to all callers.

Header contract:
- Response header name: `x-redwood-optimization-config-version`
- Value: opaque version identifier (e.g., `ocv_01JH8V3K8XW2M6H9E8RZ0G4KQY`), stable for a given immutable config version
- Only emitted when opt-in is enabled AND the underlying execution path provides...

title: Add onboarding user-agent tags for OpenAI-compat traffic attribution
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds a lightweight, privacy-safe User-Agent tagging mechanism in the OpenAI-compat proxy so we can distinguish onboarding traffic (quickstarts/templates/Console checklist flows) from general production traffic. This improves funnel attribution for TTFS/TTFP reporting for customers using OpenAI SDK...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds a lightweight, privacy-safe User-Agent tagging mechanism in the OpenAI-compat proxy so we can distinguish onboarding traffic (quickstarts/templates/Console checklist flows) from general production traffic. This improves funnel attribution for TTFS/TTFP reporting for customers using OpenAI SDKs against Redwood endpoints.

## Motivation
A meaningful portion of new Hosted API users go through the OpenAI-compatible endpoint (e.g., `openai.chat.completions.create(...)` pointed at Redwood). Today those requests are difficult to attribute to onboarding sources because the upstream UA is often generic (or absent) and we can’t reliably infer “quickstart vs prod” from payload.

We do *not* use this tag to compute checklist progress (progress remains server-side event driven per onboarding event schema/ADR); this is strictly for analytics segmentation and debugging ("is t...

title: tighten-sse-segmentation-and-async-boundary-handling-for-structured-output
preview: Motivation: We continue to see production anomalies where streamed SSE payloads emit partial JSON tokens for structured outputs (function calls / tool outputs) when the application layer triggers async flushes or when network segmentation coalesces small writes. This PR tightens segmentation logic in the OpenAI-comp...
text:
Motivation: We continue to see production anomalies where streamed SSE payloads emit partial JSON tokens for structured outputs (function calls / tool outputs) when the application layer triggers async flushes or when network segmentation coalesces small writes. This PR tightens segmentation logic in the OpenAI-compat SSE layer to ensure that emitted events are idempotent and reassemblable by downstream consumers even when mid-token boundaries are split or multiple producers race to flush.

Summary of changes: Atomic event framing tokens are introduced ("::sse-evt::") to delimit logical SSE events at the transport layer; an async boundary sanitizer defers boundary emission until the current UTF-8 token and JSON structural units are complete; a small in-memory reassembly window with sequence ids protects against out-of-order fragment delivery during concurrent flushes; and function-cal...

title: client-negotiation: graceful request/response schema negotiation + streaming control header
preview: Context: We need a less-disruptive path for rolling out the v2 request/response shape (structured outputs + richer function/tool calling) to OpenAI-compatible clients. Previously we relied on server-side feature flags and opt-in query params which made client routing and proxying brittle for streaming responses. Thi...
text:
Context: We need a less-disruptive path for rolling out the v2 request/response shape (structured outputs + richer function/tool calling) to OpenAI-compatible clients. Previously we relied on server-side feature flags and opt-in query params which made client routing and proxying brittle for streaming responses. This PR introduces a client-driven negotiation header plus a lightweight server backcompat shim and explicit streaming control header to make gradual adoption safer.

TL;DR: Add a Redwood-Schema header to let clients declare protocol intent (v1 | v2-beta | v2). Add Redwood-Stream-Mode to indicate how the server should stream structured tokens (raw-delta vs framed-json). Implement a server-side compatibility layer that inspects Redwood-Schema and falls back to canonical v1 envelopes when necessary.

Motivation:
- Reduce incidents where proxies or gateways rewrite streaming chun...
